title: solidago rigida subsp. humilis
intro: Solidago rigida subsp. humilis, also known as stiff goldenrod, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the aster family and is native to eastern North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating a range of health conditions. Solidago rigida subsp. humilis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and it has also been used in traditional herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
